prior.content_md:
*Note: This section describes the regulation's requirements as they apply to entities in the compliance chain above the workspace (fuel suppliers and aircraft operators). Understanding these obligations informs the workspace's commercial exposure and client-conversation capability, but none of these items constitute direct workspace compliance requirements.*

## SAF Blending Mandate (Fuel Suppliers)

Aviation fuel suppliers must ensure that the total volume of aviation fuel made available at each EU airport contains, at a minimum, the following SAF shares:

| Compliance Year | Minimum SAF Share | Sub-mandate: Synthetic Fuels (e-fuels/PtL) |
|---|---|---|
| From 2025 | 2% | — |
| From 2030 | 6% | 1.2% |
| From 2035 | 20% | 2% |
| From 2040 | 34% | 5% |
| From 2045 | 42% | 10% |
| From 2050 | 70% | 35% |

The mandate is assessed per airport. Suppliers are not permitted to average across airports.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 4 and Annex I, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

## Uptake Obligation (Aircraft Operators)

Aircraft operators departing from EU airports must uplift all SAF made available to them that meets the specification in the regulation. They may not refuse available SAF to circumvent the mandate.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 5, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

## SAF Definition and Eligible Feedstocks

SAF eligible under the regulation includes fuels certified under CORSIA and fuels meeting the criteria of Annex IX of the Renewable Energy Directive (RED II/III), covering advanced biofuels and synthetic fuels produced from renewable electricity. Not all fuels marketed as "sustainable" qualify; food- and feed-crop-based biofuels are expressly excluded.

**Effect on the workspace:** Airlines may produce documentation of SAF sourcing eligibility in support of client sustainability reporting. The workspace should verify that any SAF documentation provided by airline partners references eligible categories under the regulation's definitions, not broader industry sustainability claims. This is material for the workspace's clients operating under CSRD or other GHG disclosure frameworks.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 3(5) SAF definition and reference to Annex IX RED, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

## Non-Compliance Penalties

Member States must impose financial penalties on non-compliant fuel suppliers and aircraft operators. The regulation specifies that penalties must be dissuasive, proportionate, and effective, and must be at least equivalent to the market price of SAF multiplied by the volume shortfall. The specific penalty mechanism is set at Member State level.

**Effect on the workspace:** Penalty enforcement primarily affects fuel suppliers and aircraft operators, not the workspace. However, if an airline partner incurs significant penalties, this could affect its commercial stability or trigger renegotiation of cargo capacity arrangements. No direct workspace action required; monitor for enforcement developments at key EU hub airports.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 13, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---
